description: Buggy Java code cards for Bug Blackjack. Each card has 'id', 'bugCount', 'code', and 'bugs' (list of bug explanations).

cards.title: Add Two Numbers
bugs: Line 5: Uses subtraction (-) instead of addition (+) to calculate the sum.
Line 7: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 7: The closing brace } on line 8 is for main(), but there is no closing brace for the class — the class body is never closed, causing a compile error.

cards.title: Count to Ten
bugs: Line 3: Loop uses i-- (decrement) instead of i++ (increment), causing an infinite loop.
Line 6: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 7: Prints 11 as the last number but the loop only goes up to 10.

cards.title: Check Even or Odd
bugs: Line 4: Uses assignment (=) instead of equality check (==) inside the if condition.
Line 5: Prints 'odd' when the number is even — the even and odd messages are completely swapped.
Line 7: Prints 'even' when the number is odd — the even and odd messages are completely swapped.

cards.title: Simple Array Sum
bugs: Line 4: Initializes total to 1 instead of 0, making the final sum wrong.
Line 5: Loop condition uses <= instead of <, accessing one index past the end of the array and crashing.
Line 3: The array variable is named 'numbers' but the class is called 'ArraySum' — the variable should be declared as int[] array, not int[] numbers, to match the class intent.

cards.title: Multiply Two Numbers
bugs: Line 3: Missing semicolon after 'int x = 6'.
Line 5: Uses addition (+) instead of multiplication (*) to compute the result.
Line 7: Divides by 3 instead of 2 to compute half of the result.
Line 6: The print statement says 'times' but the operator on line 5 is +, so the label is a lie.

cards.title: Find the Maximum
bugs: Line 7: When a > b, assigns b (the smaller number) to max instead of a.
Line 9: When a <= b, assigns a (the smaller number) to max instead of b.
Line 12: Prints max again but labels it 'Min' — copy-pasted the wrong variable name.
Line 6: The opening parenthesis of the if condition is missing its matching ) — should be if (a > b).

cards.title: String Greeting
bugs: Line 4: Uses subtraction operator (-) instead of concatenation (+) to join two strings.
Line 6: Compares strings with == instead of .equals(), which does not reliably compare string contents in Java.
Line 9: References 'Name' (capital N) instead of 'name', causing a compile error.
Line 3: The string literal "Alice" is missing its opening double quote — should be String name = "Alice".

cards.title: Countdown Timer
bugs: Line 4-6: The while loop never decrements count, so it prints 10 forever — infinite loop.
Line 7: The string literal is missing its closing double quote before the closing parenthesis.
Line 8: After the loop, count is 0, so doubled is always 0 instead of 20.
Line 3: count is declared as an int but the loop needs to decrement it — count-- is missing entirely from the loop body.

cards.title: Rectangle Area
bugs: Line 5: Calculates area using addition (+) instead of multiplication (*).
Line 6: Perimeter formula missing parentheses — should be 2 * (width + height), not 2 * width + height.
Line 7: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 9: Uses assignment (=) instead of equality check (==) inside the comparison.
Line 9: The closing parenthesis of the println call is on the wrong side — the expression (width = height) is missing its own closing paren, making the brackets unbalanced.

cards.title: Grade Checker
bugs: Line 7: Missing opening curly brace { after the else if condition, causing a syntax error.
Line 8: Score 80-89 should give grade 'B' but the code assigns 'C'.
Line 9-10: Score 70-79 should give grade 'C' but the code assigns 'B'.
Line 11: Score below 70 is assigned 'F' but the grade 'D' (for 60-69) is completely skipped.
Line 4: The variable 'grade' is declared but never given a default value — if none of the if/else branches matched, Java would refuse to compile because grade might be uninitialized.

cards.title: Simple Calculator
bugs: Line 4: num2 is set to 0, causing division by zero on line 8.
Line 8: Dividing by 0 throws an ArithmeticException and crashes the program.
Line 9: Taking modulo with 0 also throws an ArithmeticException.
Line 10: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 1: The class is named 'Calculator' but the file would need to be saved as Calculator.java — if the filename doesn't match the class name exactly, Java refuses to compile it.

cards.title: Name Reverser
bugs: Line 5: Loop starts at name.length() which is out of bounds — should start at name.length() - 1.
Line 6: charAt(name.length()) throws a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException on the very first iteration.
Line 10: Uses assignment (=) instead of equality check (==) in the comparison.
Line 10: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 4: reversed is declared as a String but is built by += in a loop — the + operator on String is missing its left operand quote, meaning the empty string "" is typed without its closing quote.

cards.title: Temperature Converter
bugs: Line 4: Fahrenheit formula subtracts 32 instead of adding — should be (celsius * 9/5) + 32.
Line 5: Kelvin formula subtracts 273.15 instead of adding — should be celsius + 273.15.
Line 8: Uses assignment (=) instead of equality check (==) inside the boolean expression.
Line 9: Reverse formula should subtract 32 not add it — should be (fahrenheit - 32) * 5 / 9.
Line 10: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.

cards.title: Factorial Calculator
bugs: Line 4: result is initialized to 0 instead of 1 — multiplying 0 by anything is always 0.
Line 5: Loop condition is i < n instead of i <= n, so it skips multiplying by 5.
Line 8: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 10: Checks result < 0 to see if positive — that is backwards, should be result > 0.
Line 11: Uses ^ which is the bitwise XOR operator in Java, not the power operator.
Line 9: 'Double' is a Java class name — using it as a variable label in a print statement is confusing and misleading.
Line 1: The class is declared as 'public class Factorial' but is missing its opening curly brace { after the class name.

cards.title: Shopping Cart
bugs: Line 5: Uses addition (+) instead of multiplication (*) to calculate the subtotal.
Line 6: Multiplies subtotal by 10 instead of 0.10 — that is 1000% tax, not 10%.
Line 7: Subtracts tax from subtotal instead of adding it.
Line 10: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 12: Divides total by 0, throwing an ArithmeticException and crashing.
Line 12: The closing parenthesis of println is missing — the statement is never closed.
Line 2: The method signature says 'public static void main' but is missing the return type keyword void — should be 'public static void main(String[] args)'.

cards.title: Student Average
bugs: Line 5: Loop condition uses <= instead of <, accessing one index past the end of the array and crashing.
Line 6: Uses =+ instead of += — this just reassigns sum to the positive value of grades[i] each time instead of adding.
Line 8: Divides two ints so the decimal is silently chopped off — needs (double) cast.
Line 10: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 11: Uses => which is not a valid Java operator — should be >=.
Line 12: Accesses grades[10] but the array only has indices 0 through 4 — instant crash.
Line 3: The array opening brace is { but the closing brace uses ] — mismatched brackets on the array literal.

cards.title: Password Validator
bugs: Line 4: Checks length > 12 instead of >= 8 — a password with exactly 8 characters would wrongly fail.
Line 5: This returns true only when the whole password is uppercase, which means hasUpper is actually detecting 'no lowercase', the exact opposite of what the name says.
Line 6: Same inverted logic — hasLower returns true only when the whole password is lowercase, which is the opposite of what it should do.
Line 7: Missing parentheses — due to operator precedence this reads as (longEnough && hasUpper) || hasLower instead of longEnough && (hasUpper || hasLower).
Line 12: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 13: Calls password.length without parentheses — length is a method, not a field, so this does not compile.
Line 2: The method is declared as 'public static void main(String[] args)' but the opening curly brace { is on the wrong line — it belongs on this line, not indented two lines below.

cards.title: Number Guessing Game
bugs: Line 3-4: secret and guess are both 42 so the loop condition is immediately false and the loop never runs.
Line 11: References 'Secret' with a capital S instead of 'secret' — Java is case-sensitive, this does not compile.
Line 13: References 'Guess' with a capital G instead of 'guess' — same problem, does not compile.
Line 13: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 14: Divides 100 by attempts which is 0 — throws an ArithmeticException.
Line 6: The while loop has no closing brace } on line 8 — the brace that closes it is actually closing main(), leaving the loop body unterminated.
Line 5: attempts is declared as int but is used in a division on line 14 — it should be declared as double to avoid integer division truncating the result.

cards.title: Print Full Name
bugs: Line 5: Concatenates firstName and lastName with no space, producing 'JohnDoe' instead of 'John Doe'.
Line 6: References 'fullname' in all lowercase instead of 'fullName' in camelCase — Java is case-sensitive, this does not compile.
Line 7: The println statement is missing its closing parenthesis ).

cards.title: Square a Number
bugs: Line 4: Uses addition (num + num) instead of multiplication (num * num) to square the number.
Line 6: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 6: The closing brace } on line 7 is indented under println, but is actually closing main() — the class closing brace } is missing entirely.

cards.title: Swap Two Variables
bugs: Line 5: Overwrites x with y before saving x's original value — x's value (5) is lost forever.
Line 6: Assigns x back to y, but x is now 10, so both variables end up as 10.
Line 8: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.

cards.title: Print a Triangle
bugs: Line 10: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 11: rows - 1 inside string concatenation is evaluated as string + rows then - 1, which is a type mismatch — needs parentheses: (rows - 1).
Line 11: Even with fixed parentheses, the last row has 'rows' stars (5), not rows - 1 (4) — the value printed is wrong.

cards.title: Age Eligibility Check
bugs: Line 4: Uses > 18 instead of >= 18 — someone who is exactly 18 cannot vote according to this code.
Line 5: Uses > 15 instead of >= 16 — someone who is exactly 16 cannot drive according to this code.
Line 8: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 8: age * 12 is inside a string concatenation without parentheses — Java will concatenate age as a string then try to subtract 12, causing a type error.

cards.title: Circle Calculator
bugs: Line 4: pi is set to 3 instead of 3.14159 (or Math.PI) — every calculation using pi will be wrong.
Line 6: Circumference formula is missing pi — should be 2 * pi * radius.
Line 9: Prints radius as the diameter, but diameter is 2 * radius.
Line 4: pi is declared as a double but assigned the integer literal 3 with no decimal point — should be 3.14159 or Math.PI.

cards.title: FizzBuzz
bugs: Line 8: The FizzBuzz condition is checked last, but it can never be reached because the Fizz check on line 4 already catches multiples of 3 first.
Line 4: The Fizz-only check must come after the FizzBuzz check, not before.
Line 6: The Buzz-only check must also come after the FizzBuzz check, not before.
Line 8: The else if on line 8 is missing its opening curly brace { — the body of the FizzBuzz branch is not properly opened.

cards.title: Min of Three Numbers
bugs: Line 10: Prints min again but labels it 'Maximum' — maximum is never calculated.
Line 11: a + b + c inside string concatenation prints '472' instead of 13 because Java treats it as string appending — needs parentheses: (a + b + c).
Line 9: The closing parenthesis of println is missing — the statement is never properly closed.
Line 2: The method signature 'public static void main(String[] args)' is missing the square brackets on String[] — written as 'String args' instead of 'String[] args'.

cards.title: Fibonacci Sequence
bugs: Line 11: Assigns a to b, but a was just updated to b on line 10, so b is set to itself and never advances — should be b = next.
Line 9: next is calculated correctly but is completely unused — b is never set to next.
Line 8: Prints a trailing comma after every number including the last one.
Line 13: 'Done' appears on the same line as the last number because line 8 used System.out.print with no newline.
Line 7: The for loop opening brace { is missing — the loop body is not properly opened.

cards.title: Power Function
bugs: Line 5: result is initialized to 0 — multiplying 0 by base any number of times is always 0.
Line 10: Uses ^ which is bitwise XOR, not the power operator — should use base * base or Math.pow(base, 2).
Line 11: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 10: base ^ 2 is inside a string concatenation without parentheses — the ^ operator will be applied to the whole left string expression, not just base, causing a type error.
Line 3: base is declared as int but should be declared as double if the result is ever going to be cast to double — mixing int base with double cast later is inconsistent.

cards.title: String Word Count
bugs: Line 4: Counting spaces gives one fewer than the actual word count — 3 spaces means 4 words, but wordCount ends up as 3.
Line 11: References 'Sentence' with a capital S instead of 'sentence' — does not compile.
Line 12: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 5: The for loop condition uses sentence.length() but the variable is spelled sentance.length() — typo in the condition means it does not compile.
Line 12: The closing parenthesis of println is missing — the method call is never closed.

cards.title: Find Second Largest
bugs: Line 5: secondLargest starts at nums[0] — if nums[0] is the largest value, nothing will ever beat it in the else-if check.
Line 9: When a new largest is found, secondLargest is never updated to the old largest value, so it can be skipped entirely.
Line 14: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 3: The array literal uses curly braces {} but the closing bracket is written as ] instead of } — mismatched bracket.
Line 13: The opening quote of the string "Largest: " is missing — the string literal is not properly opened.

cards.title: Bank Account
bugs: Line 5: Withdrawal (1500.0) is larger than the balance after deposit (1250.0), sending the account negative with no check.
Line 9: Operator precedence causes this to print 'Balance: $-250.0false' — the < comparison needs parentheses: (balance < 0).
Line 11: newBalance is set to balance + balance (doubling the balance) instead of balance + interest.
Line 13: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 9: The closing parenthesis of println is placed before the < operator, cutting the expression short — the whole comparison must be inside println's argument.
Line 2: The method signature reads 'public static void Main' with a capital M — Java's entry point must be lowercase 'main', not 'Main'.
Line 10: interest is calculated as balance * 5 / 100 but balance is negative at this point — this produces a negative interest value which is nonsensical.

cards.title: Bubble Sort
bugs: Line 6: Inner loop goes up to j < n but accesses arr[j + 1] — on the last iteration j = n-1, so arr[n] is out of bounds and crashes.
Line 10: The swap is broken — arr[j + 1] is set to arr[j], but arr[j] was already overwritten on line 9, so arr[j + 1] gets the wrong value; should be arr[j + 1] = temp.
Line 17: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 14: The string "Sorted: " ends with a colon and space, but the sorted numbers are printed on the next line by a separate loop — the label and the output are on different lines.
Line 15: The enhanced for loop is missing its opening curly brace { — the body of the loop is not properly opened.

Line 8: temp is declared as int but arr[j] is also int — this is fine, but temp is declared inside the if block which means it is re-declared on every swap; it should be declared once outside the inner loop.
Line 1: The class name 'BubbleSort' contains no vowels in 'Bubble' — just kidding, but the class is missing its closing brace } at the very end of the file.

cards.title: Palindrome Checker
bugs: Line 8: Compares strings with == instead of .equals() — == checks if they are the same object in memory, not if they have the same characters.
Line 11: Calls word.Length() with a capital L — the correct method is word.length() with a lowercase l; this does not compile.
Line 12: Calls word.toUppercase() with a lowercase c — the correct method is word.toUpperCase() with a capital C; this does not compile.
Line 4: reversed is built by appending to a String in a loop, but the opening double quote of the empty string "" is missing — it should be String reversed = "".
Line 9: The println statement is missing its closing parenthesis ).
Line 8: The boolean variable isPalindrome is declared but its value is immediately suspect because of the == bug — the variable name itself is misleading if the comparison is wrong.

Line 5: The for loop uses i >= 0 as the stopping condition, which is correct, but the loop variable i is spelled 'l' (lowercase L) on one of the references inside the body — a typo that looks like 1 (one) and does not compile.

cards.title: Prime Number Checker
bugs: Line 4: isPrime starts as false but is set to true when a divisor is found — completely backwards; should start true and be set to false when a divisor is found.
Line 5: Loop goes up to i <= num, so it tests num % num == 0 on the last iteration, which is always true and marks every single number as non-prime.
Line 11: num + 1 inside string concatenation without parentheses prints '71' instead of 8 — needs (num + 1).
Line 12: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 14: Declares an int array of size 3 (valid indices 0, 1, 2) but line 15 stores into index 5 — instant ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
Line 10: The println statement is missing its opening parenthesis after System.out.println — written as System.out.println num + ... instead of System.out.println(num + ...).
Line 2: The method signature is missing the keyword 'static' — written as 'public void main' instead of 'public static void main', so Java cannot find the entry point.

cards.title: Matrix Diagonal Sum
bugs: Line 9: Loop condition uses <= instead of < — tries to access matrix[3][3] which does not exist, causing an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
Line 14: Divides by 4 to compute the average of the 3 diagonal elements, but there are only 3 elements — should divide by 3.
Line 15: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 3: The 2D array opening uses int[][] but the closing bracket of the outer array uses ] instead of } — mismatched brackets on the array literal.

Line 12: The string "x" between matrix.length and matrix[0].length will print something like '3x3', but if matrix were not square the two numbers could differ and the label would still say 'x' making it ambiguous.
Line 13: The string "Matrix size: " is missing its closing double quote — the string literal is not terminated.
Line 2: The method parameter is written as 'String args[]' instead of 'String[] args' — while technically valid in Java, it is the wrong style and many compilers and style checkers reject it.

cards.title: Roman Numeral Converter
bugs: Line 5: While condition is number < 0 instead of number > 0 — since number starts at 8 the loop never runs and roman stays empty.
Line 14: Uses roman.length without parentheses — length is a method not a field, so this does not compile.
Line 14: Missing semicolon at the end of the println statement.
Line 4: The empty string "" assigned to roman is missing its closing double quote — the string literal is not properly closed.
Line 12: The println statement is missing its closing parenthesis ) — the method call is never closed.
